test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM, Android, and beyond that emphasizes conciseness and null safety.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable and fault-tolerant systems.
3. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language developed by Google that powers the Flutter UI framework.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing for safer large-scale applications.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language designed for numerical and scientific computing.
6. Swift (2014) - A modern compiled language created by Apple for iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed language with Ruby-like syntax that compiles to efficient native code.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed, JVM-based language developed by JetBrains as a modern, concise alternative to Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google for building fast, cross-platform applications.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles down to plain JavaScript.
4. Elixir (2012) - A dynamic, functional language built on the Erlang VM designed for building scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance, dynamically typed language designed primarily for numerical and scientific computing.
6. Swift (2014) - Apple's compiled, general-purpose language introduced as a modern replacement for Objective-C in iOS and macOS development.
7. Hack (2014) - A statically typed dialect of PHP developed by Facebook that runs on the HipHop Virtual Machine (HHVM).

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language designed for building fast apps across multiple platforms.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional language built on the Erlang VM for scalable and fault-tolerant systems.
3. Julia (2012) - A high-performance language designed for numerical computing, data science, and scientific programming.
4. Elm (2012) - A functional language for building reliable web user interfaces.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript designed to improve large-scale application development.
6. Swift (2014) - A modern language created by Apple for building software across its platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed language with Ruby-like syntax designed for compiled performance.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Elixir (2011) - A functional concurrent language built on the Erlang VM for scalable and fault-tolerant applications.
2. Julia (2012) - A high-level high-performance dynamic language designed for scientific and numerical computing.
3. Kotlin (2012) - A statically typed language developed by JetBrains that interoperates fully with Java and targets multiple platforms.
4. Swift (2014) - A compiled general-purpose language created by Apple for developing applications across its platforms.
5. Crystal (2014) - A Ruby-inspired statically typed language that compiles to efficient native machine code.
6. Zig (2016) - A systems programming language focused on performance safety and explicit control.
7. V (2019) - A simple compiled language that prioritizes speed safety and ease of maintenance.
